fix_under_test: ws_signalr_handshake_completed gate flag + cursor field for React DOM patch keying
page_state: Something went wrong. Please try again later.

wss_observations.post_handshake_action: WSS forcibly closed (code 1006) before any chat frame transmitted
wss_observations.console_error: Error during chat: Error: WebSocket closed with status code: 1006 (no reason given)

helper_status.interpretation: APF helper NOT injected into renderer process - engine operates at network/proxy layer only, so DOM injection of warning UI cannot occur

diagnosis.iter1_handshake_gate_assessment: Gate fix did NOT resolve issue. The 'Error parsing handshake response' from #977 is gone (handshake completes 101), but engine then closes WSS with code 1006 instead of forwarding a warning frame. Cursor field addition has no effect because no DOM injection path exists - APF helper is absent in M365 Copilot renderer.
diagnosis.root_cause: M365 Copilot's SignalR-over-WSS architecture differs from typical AI services: warning injection requires a SignalR-format frame with proper invocationId/cursor mapped to React state, which the current engine cannot synthesize. Closing the connection produces a generic client-side 'Something went wrong' instead of a 차단 message.
diagnosis.comparison_to_960: #960 was FAIL_AUTH (PIN modal blocked) - resolved (no PIN modal this iteration, page logged in cleanly).
diagnosis.comparison_to_977: #977 captured HAR with 'Error parsing handshake response'. iter1 fixed the handshake parsing (handshake now completes status 101) but post-handshake behavior identical: 'Something went wrong' page, no 차단 visible. Same FAIL_NOT_RENDERED verdict, but root cause shifted from protocol-parsing to missing helper-layer DOM injection.

next_iter_recommendation: iter2 needs either (a) inject APF helper into m365.cloud.microsoft renderer to enable DOM patch path, or (b) synthesize a SignalR-format chat completion frame containing 차단 text and forward it to client instead of closing with 1006.
notes: Same FAIL_NOT_RENDERED page as #977 captured. Engine intercept clearly fires (WSS forcibly closed code 1006) but warning frame is never injected. Helper-deadlock pattern (similar to wrtn) - no APF/helper/inject keys in window, WebSocket.prototype.send is native (not hooked).
